2216	\section{Universal property of the strata}
2217	\label{sec:flatstrat_universal}
2218	
2219	\begin{theorem}
2220	
2221	\leanok
2222	[Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (case \(n = 0\))]
2223	  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}
2224	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal}
2225	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
2226	  \source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}
2227	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.
2228	  Then there exist a finite index set \(I\) and locally-closed immersions
2229	  \(\iota_f : S_f \to S\) (\(f \in I\)) with pairwise disjoint images
2230	  set-theoretically covering \(|S|\), such that the pullback of \(\F\)
2231	  along \(\iota_f\) is flat over \(S_f\) for every \(f\) (in the sense of
2232	  \cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}), and such that, writing
2233	  \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and \(i : S' \to S\) for the morphism
2234	  assembled from the \(\iota_f\), the pair \((S', i)\) has the universal
2235	  property: every morphism \(\varphi : T \to S\) for which
2236	  \(\varphi^* \F\) is flat over \(T\) factors \emph{uniquely} through
2237	  \(i\).
2238	
2239	  This is part (ii) of Nitsure's flattening-stratification theorem in the
2240	  special case \(n = 0\), stated with flatness in place of local freeness of
2241	  constant rank: for a finitely presented module the two notions agree
2242	  locally, the rank decomposing \(T\) into clopen pieces.  Nitsure states an
2243	  ``if and only if''; the converse direction --- any morphism factoring
2244	  through \(i\) has flat pullback --- is stability of flatness under base
2245	  change and is recorded separately once needed.  The universal property for
2246	  a relative family (Nitsure's part (ii) for \(\F\) on \(\PP^n_S\), hence
2247	  for projective \(\pi : X \to S\)) additionally requires the
2248	  cohomology-and-base-change layer of \cref{lem:noetherian_induction_strata}
2249	  and is not stated separately here; for a general \emph{proper} family the
2250	  universal property is not asserted by any source in the reference library,
2251	  and the reduced strata of \cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean} do not
2252	  witness it (over \(S = \Spec k[\varepsilon]\) with \(\F = \OO_S\) flat,
2253	  the identity must factor through the stratification, forcing the canonical
2254	  non-reduced stratum structure).
2255	\end{theorem}

2260	  By the case \(n = 0\) of the flattening stratification
2261	  (\cref{lem:flat_locus_open}), with its canonical scheme structure: locally
2262	  on \(S\), a basis of \(\F \otimes \kappa(s)\) prolongs by Nakayama to a
2263	  right-exact presentation
2264	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\psi} \OO_V^{\oplus e} \to \F|_V \to 0\)
2265	  on a neighbourhood \(V\) of \(s\), and the closed subscheme
2266	  \(V_e \subseteq V\) cut out by the ideal of matrix entries of \(\psi\)
2267	  represents the locus where \(\F\) becomes locally free of rank \(e\):
2268	  for \(f : T \to V\) the pulled-back presentation stays right-exact, so
2269	  \(f^*\F\) is locally free of rank \(e\) if and only if \(f^*\psi = 0\),
2270	  if and only if \(f\) factors through \(V_e\).  The local strata glue by
2271	  this representing property into locally-closed subschemes
2272	  \(S_e \subseteq S\) with \(|S_e|\) the rank-\(e\) locus; the family is
2273	  finite because the fibre rank of a coherent module is bounded on a
2274	  noetherian scheme, and the images are disjoint and cover \(|S|\) since
2275	  every point has a well-defined fibre rank.

2277	  For the universal property, let \(\varphi : T \to S\) have
2278	  \(\varphi^*\F\) flat over \(T\).  A finitely presented flat module is
2279	  locally free, and its rank is locally constant, so \(T\) decomposes into
2280	  clopen pieces \(T_e\) on which \(\varphi^*\F\) is locally free of
2281	  constant rank \(e\); by the representing property each restriction

2283	  factorizations assemble to \(\varphi = i \circ \tilde\varphi\) with
2284	  \(\tilde\varphi : T \to \coprod_e S_e\).  Uniqueness: the strata have
2285	  disjoint images, so any factorization through \(i\) induces the same

2287	  component through \(S_e\) is unique because \(\iota_e\) is an immersion,

2295	machinery is the pullback \(C \times_k T \to T\) where \(C\) is a smooth proper
2296	curve over a field \(k\) and \(T\) is any \(k\)-scheme.  The hypotheses of
2297	Nitsure's \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} demand
2298	\emph{projective} relative dimension, not merely \emph{proper}; we record that

2311	  % structurally for the Route~A reduction).
2312	  \textit{Source: classical; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.6.7 \& II.7.6 (curves).}
2313	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over a field \(k\).  Then \(C \to \Spec k\) is
2314	  projective.  Consequently, for any noetherian \(k\)-scheme \(T\), the relative
2315	  curve \(C \times_k T \to T\) is projective in the sense of Grothendieck (there
2316	  exists a relatively very ample line bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\)).
2317	\end{lemma}

2319	\begin{proof}
2320	  Projectivity of a smooth proper curve over a field is classical
2321	  ([Hartshorne], II.6.7 / II.7.6; alternatively: every divisor of large
2322	  positive degree on a smooth proper curve \(C\) is very ample, providing the
2323	  embedding \(C \hookrightarrow \PP^N_k\) for some \(N\)). Pulling the ample line
2324	  bundle back along \(C \times_k T \to C\) produces a relatively very ample line
2325	  bundle on \(C \times_k T\) over \(T\).
2326	\end{proof}

2349	  By \cref{lem:smooth_proper_curve_projective}, \(C \times_k T \to T\) is
2350	  projective, hence factors through a closed immersion into \(\PP^n_T\) for some
2351	  \(n \ge 0\).  Push \(\F\) forward along the closed immersion (extending by zero
2352	  outside the image) to a coherent sheaf \(\tilde \F\) on \(\PP^n_T\); this
2353	  operation preserves \(T\)-flatness in both directions because the closed
2354	  immersion is a finite morphism.  Apply
2355	  \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} to \(\tilde \F\) on
2356	  \(\PP^n_T\) to obtain \(\{T_f\}\) with the stated universal property.
2357	\end{proof}
2358	
2359	\section{Mathlib status}
2360	\label{sec:flatstrat_mathlib_status}
2361	
2362	\emph{Formalization status (run 0010, T12 r7).}  The Lean-typed existence
2363	statement (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification}: finite
2364	disjoint locally-closed cover with flat pullbacks, without the
2365	Hilbert-polynomial indexing) is proved sorry-free and axiom-clean, through
2366	\cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean} and the transfer lemmas of
2367	\cref{subsec:flat_transfer_strata}; the Lean sections \S3--\S6 of the
2368	statements live in \texttt{GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean} because the proof
2369	consumes generic flatness, whose glue needs the QuotScheme engine.  The
2370	\(n = 0\) stratification \texttt{flatLocusStratification}
2371	(\cref{lem:flat_locus_stratification_lean}) is likewise proved axiom-clean,
2372	derived from the existence theorem at \(\pi = \mathrm{id}_S\) through the
2373	iso-insensitivity of the flatness predicate
2374	(\texttt{coherentSheafFlat\_of\_comp\_isIso}) and empty-scheme padding of
2375	the index set.  The remaining Lean gaps in this chapter are the \(n = 0\)
2376	universal property (\texttt{flatLocusStratification\_universal},
2377	\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}, the canonical entry-ideal
2378	strata) and the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed refinement of the main statement,
2379	which additionally awaits the coherent-\(\chi\) substrate.
2380	
2381	Mathlib (master \texttt{b80f227}) provides:
2382	\begin{itemize}
2383	  \item \texttt{Module.Flat} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic}) --- the
2384	    module-theoretic notion of flatness, applicable affine-locally to the
2385	    stalks \(\F_x\).
2386	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat} ---
2387	    \texttt{IsFlat} as a morphism property and basic stability lemmas; this is
2388	    flatness of the structure morphism, \emph{not} flatness of a quasi-coherent
2389	    module over the base.
2390	  \item \texttt{Module.FaithfullyFlat} and the descent infrastructure that
2391	    consumes it.
2392	\end{itemize}
2393	
2394	\noindent What Mathlib does \emph{not} yet have, and that this chapter will
2395	introduce in the new file
2396	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}:
2397	\begin{itemize}
2398	  \item A predicate \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat}
2399	    capturing \(S\)-flatness of a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module on a finite-type
2400	    \(S\)-scheme (\cref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}). This is the
2401	    sheaf-level lift of \texttt{Module.Flat} via the affine-local
2402	    characterization in the second paragraph of the definition.
2403	  \item The constructive existence proof
2404	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification}
2405	    (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}) from Nitsure~\S4, proved
2406	    through \texttt{flatLocusReduction}
2407	    (\cref{lem:flat_locus_reduction_lean}, the Noetherian-induction
2408	    reduction via generic flatness) and packaged with injective labels by
2409	    \texttt{flatLocusAssembly} (\cref{lem:flat_locus_assembly_lean}); the
2410	    \(n = 0\) case \texttt{flatLocusStratification}
2411	    (\cref{lem:flat_locus_stratification_lean}) is derived back from the
2412	    existence theorem at \(\pi = \mathrm{id}_S\).
2413	  \item The universal-property packaging
2414	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification\_universal}
2415	    (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_universal}), bundling the \(n = 0\)
2416	    stratification with the unique factorisation of morphisms with flat
2417	    pullback through the stratum coproduct \(\coprod_f S_f \to S\); the
2418	    projective-family upgrade needs \(\PP^n_S\) vocabulary and
2419	    cohomology-and-base-change, and an eventual
2420	    \texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy}-style refinement remains
2421	    future work.
2422	  \item The dévissage layer of the algebraic generic-freeness lemma
2423	    (\cref{sec:generic_freeness_devissage}) is formalized and proved
2424	    (splicing, torsion base case, transport, prime-filtration assembly
2425	    \texttt{GenericFreeness.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}); the sole remaining
2426	    algebraic gap is the normalisation core
2427	    (\cref{lem:generically_free_domain_core}).
2428	  \item The relative-curve specialisation
2429	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve}
2430	    (\cref{cor:flattening_stratification_curve}) for the Route~A
2431	    consumer.
2432	\end{itemize}
